100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 28, 1926, in a significant development for British politics, Prime Minister Stanley Baldwin announced his government's plans to address the ongoing economic challenges and rising unemployment in the wake of World War I. In the Middle East, tensions escalated as Arab leaders rallied against British rule in Palestine, igniting fears of unrest. Meanwhile, in the United States, the Senate was set to debate a controversial bill aimed at restricting immigration, reflecting growing nativist sentiments across the nation.
